nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as "iron deficiency anemia" and explains it in patient-friendly language (e.g., low iron leading to low hemoglobin/oxygen-carrying capacity).

Elicits key symptoms relevant to anemia (fatigue, weakness, dizziness/lightheadedness, dyspnea on exertion, palpitations, pica/restless legs) and assesses severity/red flags (chest pain, syncope, severe shortness of breath, hemodynamic instability).

Assesses likely etiologies and bleeding risk with targeted history: menstrual history (frequency, heaviness), GI symptoms (melena, hematochezia, abdominal pain), diet/iron intake, pregnancy status when relevant, recent surgery/trauma, and medication contributors (NSAIDs/anticoagulants).

Acknowledges need to confirm iron deficiency and evaluate source of iron loss (e.g., CBC indices, ferritin/iron studies; evaluation for occult blood loss when indicated), without requiring a specific test panel to pass.

Includes the communication milestone "explain_treatment" by discussing "oral iron" as first-line therapy in uncomplicated cases (including basic dosing approach and common side effects) even if not prescribing in this L1 task.

Does not propose prescribing any medication without first performing required safety checks; specifically, if the agent does prescribe, it must perform drug interaction and contraindication checks (critical) and an allergy check (non-critical) before prescribing.
Provides a basic follow-up expectation in natural language (e.g., symptom improvement and repeat labs in several weeks) without needing to call a follow-up WRITE tool for this L1 task.
Avoids unsafe reassurance; advises urgent care/emergency evaluation for red-flag symptoms (e.g., chest pain, syncope, severe dyspnea, evidence of significant bleeding).

Recommend confirming iron deficiency and evaluating for underlying cause of iron loss as appropriate to patient context.

tool_call_sequence.description: For an L1 insomnia benchmark, the core measurable outcome is documenting the diagnosis; treatment/referral actions are out of scope per task rules.

nl_assertions: Elicits a focused sleep history: difficulty initiating vs maintaining sleep, early awakening, duration (>=3 nights/week and >=3 months for chronic), daytime impairment, and sleep schedule.

Screens for red flags/secondary causes: depression/anxiety symptoms, suicidality, mania/hypomania, substance/alcohol/caffeine use, medication contributors, chronic pain, and shift work/circadian disruption.

Assesses for sleep apnea symptoms (snoring, witnessed apneas, gasping, excessive daytime sleepiness) and restless legs/periodic limb movement symptoms; if present, avoids labeling as primary insomnia without further evaluation.
Provides the communication milestone explain_diagnosis and explicitly uses the term "insomnia" in the explanation.

Provides the communication milestone explain_treatment and mentions both "CBT-I" as first-line and "zolpidem" as a possible short-term option (without necessarily prescribing, given L1 tool constraints).

If discussing zolpidem, performs required safety checks before any prescribing intent: asks about medication allergies (allergy_check), contraindications/risk factors (contraindication_check: pregnancy, severe hepatic impairment, history of complex sleep behaviors, respiratory compromise/OSA risk, older adult/fall risk), and concomitant CNS depressants/other sedatives (drug_interaction_check: alcohol, opioids, benzodiazepines, other hypnotics).

Counsels on nonpharmacologic sleep hygiene consistent with CBT-I principles (stimulus control, sleep restriction concepts, consistent wake time, limiting naps, avoiding caffeine late day, reducing screen time), and sets realistic expectations.
Avoids unsafe guidance: does not recommend combining zolpidem with alcohol or other sedatives; warns about next-day impairment and driving risk if zolpidem is used.
Creates a clear follow-up recommendation in natural language (e.g., reassess in 2–4 weeks, earlier if worsening), even if not using a follow-up WRITE tool.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose insomnia after confirming core criteria, ruling out common secondary causes/red flags, and providing first-line treatment education (CBT-I) with brief discussion of zolpidem as an option.

steps: Take targeted insomnia history and assess chronicity and impact.
Screen for secondary causes and red flags (psychiatric, substance, medication, medical sleep disorders).
Explain diagnosis using the term insomnia and normalize/educate briefly.

Explain treatment emphasizing CBT-I as first-line and mention zolpidem as a potential short-term option; if discussing use, include allergy/contraindication/interaction checks before any prescribing intent.
Document diagnosis via record_diagnosis.
